Current error: Expected { premium: 165 }, received { premium: 115 }
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Note: Curse adds 50% surcharge on the item's base premium. Update Item type to include optional cursed flag.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to support the cursed flag with 50% surcharge.

content.text:
All 13 tests pass (the new cursed sword test plus the 12 existing tests).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added optional `cursed?: boolean` flag to the Item type and an `itemBasePremium` helper that adds a 50% surcharge to the item's base when cursed.
- Result: All tests now pass (13 passing, 36 todo).
- Approach: Minimal change. Reused existing `BASE_PREMIUMS` lookup, just wrapped it with a conditional that adds 50% when cursed. The existing block-of-three logic still applies via `sumIndividualBasePremiums`, but cursed items don't enter blocks in the current test scope. No future-test logic added.

Verification of minimalism:
- Did not touch claim function.
- Did not change block logic for cursed items (no test demands it yet).
- Did not introduce enchantment, dragon-material, or other modifiers.
- Used simplest expression: `base + base * 0.5`.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_03-35-32_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_03-35-32_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
